_comment: Durable lookup table for the anchored ladder (tools/anchored_ladder.csv) and pool-building automation (tools/build_pool_from_ladder.py). Maps ladder node names to B2 checkpoint keys (bbr:bloodbowl-rl/checkpoints/<b2_key>), local training/ filenames, and one-line provenance. All entries are flat-fp32 CUDA blobs, 16066560 bytes (obs-v4, hidden 512x3, heads 30/33/391). Updated D111.
_decisions: DECISIONS.md D90-D122 (contested era through hard-opponents doctrine; D122 = league6/6b generalization verdict + reward-probe placement, elo_d122 fields)

bc_v4.status: degenerate (0% decisive vs everything, D98) -- floor candidate ONLY for BC-prior style diversity, no training signal

kickoff8.lineage: v4 dead-teacher-era kickoff ladder, final (D92, the 9th and last dead-teacher record, tds 1.438)
kickoff8.status: diversity candidate -- old-era style, weak but stable

gen1.status: FLOOR anchor -- stable, non-degenerate reference point (anchor=gen1 in all ladder fits)

exploiter2.lineage: H3 AlphaStar-exploiter, champion=exploiter1, warm=gen4 (D103, flat 0.504 vs champion)
exploiter2.status: diversity candidate -- weak overall but non-transitive (beats league4 despite -93.6 Elo gap, D107)

exploiter4.lineage: H3 AlphaStar-exploiter, champion=league4, warm=gen1 (D108, flat 0.367 vs champion)
exploiter4.status: diversity candidate -- marginal vs gen1 warm-start, non-transitive (beats gen2/gen3 despite ~85-90 Elo gap, D108)

exploiter3.lineage: H3 AlphaStar-exploiter, champion=exploiter1, warm=league2 (D106, flat 0.493 vs champion)

gen2cont1.lineage: ratchet-continuation diagnostic, TEACHER=WARM=gen2, exact gen2->gen3 recipe repeated (D110, regressed -57.3 Elo from gen2)
gen2cont1.status: diversity candidate -- NOT viable as a warm-start/champion (D110: ratchet-vs-frozen-self from a tier1 checkpoint regresses), but the checkpoint itself remains a valid diversity pool member

league2.lineage: league pool gen2, warm=league1, skillup exposure probe (D96-A/D99, lost to predecessor league1)
league2.status: diversity candidate -- pool member of league5/6/6b

exploiter1.lineage: H3 AlphaStar-exploiter, champion=gen2, warm=league1 (D102, SUCCESS: 0.587 vs champion, the only exploiter win)

league4.lineage: league pool gen4, warm=league3 (D106, partial recovery from league2's dip, still below league1)
league4.status: TIER2 -- superseded as a pool member by league5 in league6/6b (D109-A)

gen4.lineage: v4-contested ratchet gen4, warm=teacher=gen3 (D100, landed within/below {gen2,gen3,league1} cluster -- ratchet plateau confirmed)

league1.lineage: league pool gen1, warm=v4-contested gen1 cap, first rotating-pool arm (D96, dead heat vs gen2-ratchet at matched steps)
league1.status: TIER2 -- the league lineage's high-water-mark BEFORE league5 (league2-4 all below it)

gen3.lineage: v4-contested ratchet gen3, warm=teacher=gen2 (D97/D98, the original tier1-tie with gen2 -- 6.1 Elo apart, noise-band)
gen3.status: TIER1/hard-opponent candidate -- pool member of league5/6/6b; warm-start for league6b (D109-A)

gen2.lineage: v4-contested ratchet gen2, warm=teacher=gen1 (D95, the original +100 Elo jump, gen1->gen2)
gen2.status: TIER1/hard-opponent candidate -- pool member of league5/6/6b

league5.lineage: league pool gen5, warm=league4, FIRST pool to include gen2+gen3 as opponents (D106-A)
league5.status: TIER1/hard-opponent -- warm-start for league6 (D109-A); the result that motivated D111's doctrine. Was ALL-TIME #1 at D109; superseded at D122 by statmatch1/league6/tdmaniac1 on the extended ladder (still solidly top-4).

league6.lineage: league pool gen6, warm=league5 (D109-A continuation, SAME D111 hard-opponents pool {gen2,gen3,league5,gen4,league2,gen1,exploiter1,kickoff8}). Capped at 28.03B/30B (D116, PufferLib post-train KeyError ate the last 2B).
league6.status: TIER1/hard-opponent -- league5 continuation that JUMPED +48.7 Elo above league5 (D122, +69.7 over league5's D109 value), confirming the D111 recipe keeps ratcheting. Beats league5 0.529 H2H. Best non-reward-probe checkpoint on the extended ladder; recommended warm-start/champion (used as warm for TD-maniac, D116-A).

league6b.lineage: league pool, warm=gen3 (D109-A generalization arm, SAME D111 pool as league6 but different starting lineage). Capped at 28.03B/30B (D116, PufferLib post-train KeyError ate the last 2B).
league6b.status: TIER1/hard-opponent -- D109-A GENERALIZATION ARM. Starting from gen3 (+95.8) it climbed to +156.6 (D122, +60.8 over gen3), clearly above the gen2/gen3 cluster, confirming the D111 hard-opponents recipe generalizes across starting lineages. But it lands BELOW league5 (loses 0.461 H2H) and league6 (loses 0.452): the jump from gen3 is real but smaller than league5's from league4 -- starting state matters, the recipe is not lineage-invariant in magnitude.

tdmaniac1.lineage: reward-economy probe (D116-A, 15B), warm/teacher=league6, reward_possession 0 + reward_td 1.2 + dist_endzone 0.3, frozen-self kickoff-pure (no D111 pool).
tdmaniac1.status: TIER1/hard-opponent -- the reward-td-maniac probe lands #3 on the extended ladder (+215.6), between league6 and league5. Loses to statmatch1 (0.553) but beats league5/league6b H2H. A strong checkpoint; reward-td-up did not hurt tournament strength.

stakes1.lineage: reward-economy probe (D116-B, 15B), warm/teacher=league6b, reward_win 1.4 (win/loss spread 1.2->2.8), reward_possession left ON, frozen-self kickoff-pure (no D111 pool).
stakes1.status: TIER1/hard-opponent -- raised-stakes probe lands +190.5 (just below league5). Beats league6b (its warm-start, 0.516 H2H) and the gen2/gen3 cluster; loses to statmatch1/tdmaniac1/league6. Top-5 strong checkpoint.

statmatch1.lineage: aggregate-stat-matching reward probe (D114-A, 5B only), warm=league5, reward_possession 0 + reward-statmatch-scale 0.01, frozen-self kickoff-pure (no D111 pool). Produced on the rig with a --float build; flat-fp32 format loads in puffer match with NO conversion (verified D122).
statmatch1.status: TIER1/hard-opponent -- NEW ALL-TIME LADDER #1 (+326.5) despite only 5B steps. Beats EVERY opponent decisively incl. league5 (0.543), league6 (0.513), tdmaniac1 (0.553), stakes1 (0.600). Consistent across all 12 pairings (not a weak-opponent artifact). The aggregate-stat-matching reward is the single strongest lever measured. CAVEAT: 5B-only, warm=league5; high decisive win-rate vs weak nodes (bc_v4 0.883) does inflate BT strength somewhat, but top-cluster H2H wins confirm the #1 placement is real.
